AtlasHomeWorkCard.title: AtlasHomeWorkCard
AtlasHomeWorkCard.description: One active Vesper-authored Atlas work card for the home surface.

AtlasInboxItem.title: AtlasInboxItem
AtlasInboxItem.description:
One row in the Memory Inbox.

V0 surfaces only pending candidates and recent artifacts. V1 adds the
"add more photos" prompts, pattern cards, and artifact anniversaries.

AtlasInboxPage.title: AtlasInboxPage
AtlasInboxPage.description: Paginated inbox response. ``next_cursor`` is null when no more rows.

AtlasInboxReviewItem.title: AtlasInboxReviewItem
AtlasInboxReviewItem.description:
Backend-authored Atlas Inbox review item.

These are the broad Atlas review queue rows: memories, patterns, places,
postcards, corrections, and signal reviews. The frontend renders these
directly instead of inferring Vesper's queue from unrelated hooks.

AtlasOnThisDayItem.title: AtlasOnThisDayItem
AtlasOnThisDayItem.description:
A memory whose month/day matches today, from a past year.

The honest, recurring source of "aliveness" on quiet days — no
manufactured activity, just real anniversaries of trips/artifacts.

AtlasPlacesReadModel.title: AtlasPlacesReadModel
AtlasPlacesReadModel.description: Response for ``GET /api/atlas/places`` — saved venues + memory clusters.

AtlasScanHistory.title: AtlasScanHistory
AtlasScanHistory.description: One-row-per-user scan tracking. Updated after every successful scan.

AtlasSignalStateUpdate.title: AtlasSignalStateUpdate
AtlasSignalStateUpdate.description: PATCH body for user control over a single derived signal.

archive_reason.title: Archive Reason
archive_reason.description: Why the row is archived when ``visibility`` is ``archived``. ``dedup`` / ``stale`` / ``superseded`` are system-initiated; ``user`` is a direct PATCH to ``archived``. User hides via ``POST …/hide`` set ``visibility=hidden`` and leave this null.

AtlasTimelineEntry.title: AtlasTimelineEntry
AtlasTimelineEntry.description:
One normalised row in the Atlas Timeline.

``id`` is the persisted projection row UUID (``atlas_timeline_entries.id``).
``source_type`` / ``source_id`` link back to the canonical row.
``occurred_start`` / ``occurred_end`` are when the travel happened;
``surfaced_at`` is when Vesper noticed or generated it.

AtlasTimelineEntryUpdate.title: AtlasTimelineEntryUpdate
AtlasTimelineEntryUpdate.description: PATCH body for user display overrides on a persisted timeline row.

AtlasTimelineGroup.title: AtlasTimelineGroup
AtlasTimelineGroup.description: A single month bucket inside the Almanac read model.

AtlasTimelineRemovedSummary.title: AtlasTimelineRemovedSummary
AtlasTimelineRemovedSummary.description: Counts for hidden and archived projection rows.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: URL of the image to rehost.

curator_ref.title: Curator Ref
curator_ref.description: Curator identifier, e.g. 'curator:alex'. Free-form for now.

AuditEvent.title: AuditEvent
AuditEvent.description: One audit-trail entry. Designed to be human-readable in a settings UI.

BetterSlot.title: BetterSlot
BetterSlot.description: A suggested move target for a block (itinerary-editing.md §5.2 BETTER SLOTS).

BlockAlternative.title: BlockAlternative
BlockAlternative.description:
One Swap candidate for an itinerary block (itinerary-editing.md §6).

A pre-reasoned recommendation, not a raw search hit: ``fit_line`` and
``cost_delta`` let the UI explain why it's offered. ``cost_delta`` is the
candidate's ``price_per_person_estimate`` minus the current venue's, in the
same units the venue stores (signed; negative = cheaper).

BlockAlternativesResponse.title: BlockAlternativesResponse
BlockAlternativesResponse.description:
``GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/itinerary/blocks/{block_id}/alternatives``.

The CandidateSet for a block's Swap surface. ``reason`` is set (and
``candidates`` empty) when there's nothing to offer — e.g. ``no_venue``
for a free-time / experience block.

BlockTransition.title: BlockTransition
BlockTransition.description:
How the group moves from this block to the next.

``mode`` + ``minutes`` are the canonical pair; ``rationale`` is the
one-line "why this way" that lets the UI explain pacing choices.
``distance_km`` is optional — the planner emits it when it's useful
context (long taxi, cross-city train). Booking-specific keys
(``origin``, ``destination``, ``pickup_location``) still live here as
extras so the booking agent's existing readers keep working.

BookingActionResponse.title: BookingActionResponse
BookingActionResponse.description: Shared response model for booking mutation endpoints.

BridgeRenderRequest.title: BridgeRenderRequest
BridgeRenderRequest.description: Render a bridge sentence to audio for inline playback.

BridgeRenderResponse.title: BridgeRenderResponse
BridgeRenderResponse.description:
Inline base64 audio data URL for the bridge.

Phase 6b.3 v1 returns the audio inline so the client can hand
it directly to ``adapter.setSource()`` without an extra round-trip.
Phase 8 can switch to CDN URLs once R2 is wired and bridge audio
starts being cached server-side.

CaptureMomentRequest.title: CaptureMomentRequest
CaptureMomentRequest.description:
Body for POST /api/trips/{tid}/observations.

The capture-nudge card surfaces a soft prompt at evening-during-trip;
the user types a short reflection and submits. The body is text-only
in Phase 4 — photo capture lands in Phase 5 once the media pipeline
(S3 / CDN / size limits) is in place.

``content`` is the captured text. ``importance`` is exposed so future
surfaces (voice agent transcribed memories, multi-line journal) can
bias persistence; the home capture-nudge passes the default 5.

Caveat.title: Caveat
Caveat.description:
Structured because caveats get special UI treatment on the phone —
they can't be buried in the body (allergen warnings, accessibility, etc.).

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Stable per-device identifier from the client

CollectionDetail.title: CollectionDetail
CollectionDetail.description: Full collection payload: metadata + ordered hydrated members.

CollectionSummary.title: CollectionSummary
CollectionSummary.description: Guides-row card payload: just what the row needs to render.

ConciergeCardFeedbackRequest.title: ConciergeCardFeedbackRequest
ConciergeCardFeedbackRequest.description: Structured feedback for one Concierge Home card.

ConciergeCardLifecycleRequest.title: ConciergeCardLifecycleRequest
ConciergeCardLifecycleRequest.description: Lifecycle transition for a durable Vesper Home card.

ConflictReceipt.title: ConflictReceipt
ConflictReceipt.description: ConflictRow — two overlapping blocks (Urgent · problem / Orient · clash).

ConversationListItem.title: ConversationListItem
ConversationListItem.description:
Lightweight conversation for list views.

Enriched fields are populated by ``get_conversation_list_for_user``
so the history surface can render last-message preview + unread
count without follow-up fetches per row. Older clients that don't
need the enrichment still receive the legacy shape — the new
fields default to None / 0 so a Pydantic decode against existing
snapshots stays happy.

planning_intent_bootstrap.description: Optional onboarding draft to seed intent_state on create. Used by the talk-it-through path so Vesper can promote without re-asking for fields the user already supplied.

CreateSideChatRequest.title: CreateSideChatRequest
CreateSideChatRequest.description: Create a personal side conversation linked to a trip.

CreateSideChatResponse.title: CreateSideChatResponse
CreateSideChatResponse.description: Response after creating a side chat.

destination.title: Destination
destination.description: Human-readable destination label captured when a client starts a trip from destination-led UI. Persisted into trip_summary.

CrossTripThreadPriorTripView.title: CrossTripThreadPriorTripView
CrossTripThreadPriorTripView.description: Per-prior-trip chip data hydrated at read time on the API edge.

CrossTripThreadView.title: CrossTripThreadView
CrossTripThreadView.description:
API response shape — hydrates prior_trip_ids into title chips
so the frontend doesn't need a second fetch per chip.

DataExportResponse.title: DataExportResponse
DataExportResponse.description:
A DSAR data-access bundle: every row we hold that belongs to the
user, grouped by table. Binary fields are summarized, not dumped.

DeckColumn.title: DeckColumn
DeckColumn.description: One column in a Comparison deck face.

DeckOption.title: DeckOption
DeckOption.description: One option in a Vote or Flight deck face.

DeckPerson.title: DeckPerson
DeckPerson.description: One person row — voter in Vote/Flight, counterparty in Settle.

DeckStructured.title: DeckStructured
DeckStructured.description:
Discriminated structured payload for rich Deck card faces.

Cards without this field render the generic prose face. Cards with it
switch on ``layout`` to render the appropriate face component. All
display values are pre-formatted by the backend (e.g. "€128") so the
FE never re-derives money or colour hex values.

DeleteAccountResponse.title: DeleteAccountResponse
DeleteAccountResponse.description:
Summary of a completed account deletion — per-table row counts plus
how many shared resources were handed off to other members.

DerivedSignal.title: DerivedSignal
DerivedSignal.description:
One taste signal extracted from an artifact. Visible in the audit bridge.

See the V0 plan's Signal extraction section for the provenance contract:
every signal carries a back-reference to its source artifact, plus an
optional ``observation_id`` linking to the underlying observation row.

DigestRetryRequest.title: DigestRetryRequest
DigestRetryRequest.description:
Targeted retry for trips whose background digest failed.

``trip_ids`` is the list of UUIDs returned by
``run_digest_for_active_trips`` in its ``failed_trip_ids`` field —
typically surfaced in the warning log and passed directly here by ops.

``date`` overrides the target date; defaults to yesterday (the same
date the background loop would have used).  Pass an ISO date string
if you need to backfill a specific day.

DigestRunResponse.title: DigestRunResponse
DigestRunResponse.description: Result of a manual digest run.

DismissCardRequest.title: DismissCardRequest
DismissCardRequest.description:
Body for POST /api/trips/{tid}/home_cards/dismiss.

Identifies the card by its (archetype, ref_id) tuple — the same shape
the GET response surfaces. ``ref_id`` is TEXT to accommodate place
slugs alongside UUIDs.

DismissCardResponse.title: DismissCardResponse
DismissCardResponse.description:
Acknowledgment payload — the frontend uses ``status`` to drive the
undo toast. ``dismissed`` is the tuple just affected so the client
has everything it needs to call the undo endpoint without re-deriving
keys.

DnaConfidence.title: DnaConfidence
DnaConfidence.description: How sure the agent is about the DNA dimensions. Powers provisional copy.

DnaPhraseDisputeRequest.title: DnaPhraseDisputeRequest
DnaPhraseDisputeRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/me/dna-phrases/dispute``.

The user tapped "not quite" on a phrase rendered by the Me-tab
Travel DNA card. ``phrase`` is the exact rendered string so the
observation can carry the user-visible text into Personal Memory
synthesis verbatim.

DossierExemplarItem.title: DossierExemplarItem
DossierExemplarItem.description:
A concrete entity (venue/experience) referenced by a format-aware dossier.

Used to render inline save cards in the spot/experience reader.

EntityTake.title: EntityTake
EntityTake.description: A cached Take row — what the DB stores and the API returns.

ExpenseShareInput.title: ExpenseShareInput
ExpenseShareInput.description: Used in ExpenseCreate/Update to define shares inline.

text.title: Text
text.description: The user's free-text feedback. 4000-char limit matches App Store What's-New.

screen.title: Screen
screen.description: Screen path the user was on when they tapped Send Feedback (e.g. 'trip-home', 'me/memory').

trip_id.title: Trip Id
trip_id.description: Trip the user was viewing, if any. Helps reproduce.

GeoPoint.title: GeoPoint
GeoPoint.description: Single map point — a photo cluster centroid or named place.

GeofenceEvent.title: GeofenceEvent
GeofenceEvent.description:
One geofence trigger the server observed.

Append-only: every row represents a moment in time, immutable after
insert. ``manually_narrated`` is the catch-all for cases where the
user tapped the entity card without ever entering its geofence — we
still record it so the observability dashboard reflects total
narration attempts, geofence-driven or not.

GlobalDossierFeedResponse.title: GlobalDossierFeedResponse
GlobalDossierFeedResponse.description: Random cross-city dossier sample for the global Discover feed.

HandoffNarrationContext.title: HandoffNarrationContext
HandoffNarrationContext.description:
The context block the voice agent reads before its first turn.

Includes what the user heard, what was about to play, and the
trigger transcript — enough to ground the agent's reply without
a separate context fetch.

HeadingBlock.title: HeadingBlock
HeadingBlock.description:
A section heading. ``level`` is 2 or 3 — we deliberately don't
expose h1 because the Take headline is the de facto h1 of the card.

active_in_chat.title: Active In Chat
active_in_chat.description: Is the chat screen foregrounded right now?

headphones_connected.title: Headphones Connected
headphones_connected.description: Any audio output that's not the phone speaker?

motion.title: Motion
motion.description: Coarse activity: still / walking / running / cycling / driving / unknown

ambient_db.title: Ambient Db
ambient_db.description: Optional ambient noise estimate in dB

trip_id.title: Trip Id
trip_id.description: Trip the user is currently engaged with, if any.

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Stable per-device identifier; required when trip_id is set.

HeartbeatRequest.title: HeartbeatRequest
HeartbeatRequest.description:
Compact physical-context signal from the client.

All fields optional — clients may have permission for some signals
but not others (e.g., motion without microphone for ambient_db).

HeroIdentity.title: HeroIdentity
HeroIdentity.description: The one-line identity: place + the single most relevant status fact.

HeroRead.title: HeroRead
HeroRead.description: The one read in Vesper's voice. ``prose`` is the only LLM-authored field.

HeroStep.title: HeroStep
HeroStep.description: The one step — a door, never a transaction.

temporal_anchor.title: Temporal Anchor
temporal_anchor.description: Compact temporal cue — 'Day 3 of 5' / 'in 11 days' / '5 days ago'.

phase.title: Phase
phase.description: Calendar-relative phase for home copy and ambient framing: ideation | pre | during | post — from ``compute_phase_info(trip)`` (today vs trip start/end). Intentionally different from ``TripMapState.phase`` on the map read model, which is trip-status-relative (ideation | planning | final_prep | active | completed) and answers lifecycle/booking readiness rather than “where are we on the calendar?”.

HomeAnchor.title: HomeAnchor
HomeAnchor.description: Single always-present orientation card at the top of the feed.

archetype.title: Archetype
archetype.description: Discriminator for render + tap routing. Active zone: 'pending_decision' | 'morning_brief' | 'booking_event' | 'constraint_meal' | 'capture_nudge' | 'just_accepted' | 'pending_too_long' | 'unread_intake' | 'planning_brief' | 'trip_retrospective' | 'settlement_closeout'. Ambient zone: 'happening_nearby' | 'place_context' | 'memory_highlight' | 'next_trip_inspo'. The legacy 'group_state' alias is still emitted for one release of back-compat.

ref_id.title: Ref Id
ref_id.description: UUID-as-string for the underlying entity (proposal / experience / digest), or place slug for place_context.

day_number.title: Day Number
day_number.description: 1-based itinerary day index when the card naturally targets one day (e.g. morning digest). Omit when trip-wide or not applicable.

tier.title: Tier
tier.description: Phase 3 urgency tier driving in-zone ranking and dismissal rules. 1 = urgent / blocking (non-dismissable), 2 = this trip, 3 = informational / ambient. Phase 3 sets tier statically per archetype; deadline-driven dynamic promotion arrives in Phase 5.

HomeCard.title: HomeCard
HomeCard.description:
One follow-on card. Archetype discriminates render + tap target.

Phase 2 archetypes:
- ``pending_decision`` — open proposal awaiting group input (active)
- ``morning_brief`` — today's digest restated as a card (active)
- ``happening_nearby`` — taste-DNA-ranked experience for the destination (ambient)
- ``place_context`` — curated destination angle worth knowing (ambient)

active.title: Active
active.description: Cards needing user attention — decisions, briefings.

ambient.title: Ambient
ambient.description: Quieter discovery/context cards — happenings, place angles.

HomeFeed.title: HomeFeed
HomeFeed.description:
Full payload for ``GET /trips/{trip_id}/home_cards``.

Three zones; clients render top-to-bottom: anchor, active, ambient.

HoodReceipt.title: HoodReceipt
HoodReceipt.description: Hood — one coarse neighbourhood character read (Match).

HydratedMember.title: HydratedMember
HydratedMember.description:
One member with its entity type tag + lightweight entity payload.

The entity payload is shaped by the DB layer; the shape varies per
entity type (a venue has `name`, a dossier has `angle`, etc.). The
mobile app switches on `entity_type` to render.

Hypothesis.title: Hypothesis
Hypothesis.description:
Internal-only. The LLM's working claim about the user and its evidence.

NOT surfaced to the user. Used for:
- Debugging + eval ("why did the Take emphasize X?")
- Offline analysis (aggregating which hypothesis categories correlate
  with saves vs dismisses, feeding Personal Memory refresh)
- Grounding validation (post-generation check that every hypothesis
  references real evidence)

`evidence_refs` are opaque ID strings scoped by `evidence_type`:
- personal_memory: the Personal Memory version id or a section marker
- past_trip: trip_id
- angle_affinity: angle_id
- group_signal: group_profile_version id
- saved_entity: entity_id previously saved

IntakePayload.title: IntakePayload
IntakePayload.description:
Bulk chip payload for minimal onboarding.

Each field is optional. Sections use replace-within-section semantics:
submitting ``dietary=["vegan"]`` deactivates any other active dietary rows
for the user. Omitting a field leaves that section untouched, so callers
can update one section without disturbing others. An empty list clears
the section.

entity_id.title: Entity Id
entity_id.description: The narrated entity's id (str or uuid)

playback_position_ms.title: Playback Position Ms
playback_position_ms.description: Position into the audio when the event fired. Null for dismissed/interrupted.

trip_phase.title: Trip Phase
trip_phase.description: pre_trip | during_trip | post_trip — set by client when known

audio_position_ms.title: Audio Position Ms
audio_position_ms.description: Where in the audio the user was when they interrupted.

trigger_transcript.title: Trigger Transcript
trigger_transcript.description: The user's speech that triggered the interrupt — handed to the voice agent so it can respond directly to what was asked.

InterruptedNarrationContext.title: InterruptedNarrationContext
InterruptedNarrationContext.description: What the client knows about the narration that was interrupted.

InviteConversationSummary.title: InviteConversationSummary
InviteConversationSummary.description:
Landing-page facts for a conversation-scoped invite (pre-trip).

Lighter than :class:`InviteTripSummary` — the conversation is still
exploratory, so we render what the intent scratchpad has captured
so far rather than committed trip details.

InviteIntakeRequest.title: InviteIntakeRequest
InviteIntakeRequest.description: Chip answers and optional contact info captured pre-auth.

InviteListItem.title: InviteListItem
InviteListItem.description:
Serializable summary of one invite row, returned by list endpoints.

Used by the organizer's "Pending invites" UI — each item carries
enough state to render a status pill (sent / answered / joined /
revoked / expired) and the small momentum signal of a chip answer
when the recipient has already engaged pre-auth.

InviteSnapshot.title: InviteSnapshot
InviteSnapshot.description: Haiku-generated landing-page content for one invite view.

InviteTripSummary.title: InviteTripSummary
InviteTripSummary.description: Non-generated facts shown on the landing page alongside the snapshot.

ItineraryBlockOut.title: ItineraryBlockOut
ItineraryBlockOut.description: API response shape for a single itinerary block.

ItineraryDayOut.title: ItineraryDayOut
ItineraryDayOut.description: API response shape for a single itinerary day with embedded blocks.

LatencyDistribution.title: LatencyDistribution
LatencyDistribution.description:
Percentile + spread summary for one latency series.

All values are integer milliseconds — the underlying column is an
INTEGER and we explicitly cast back so a float-formatted ``stddev``
doesn't leak into the response (would surprise frontend code that
keys off integer ms throughout).

LeaseInfo.title: LeaseInfo
LeaseInfo.description: Common shape for claim / get_active responses.

LeaveByActionResponse.title: LeaveByActionResponse
LeaveByActionResponse.description: Confirmation envelope for leave-by dismiss / mute actions.

LensGroup.title: LensGroup
LensGroup.description: All published angles under one travel lens, for the editorial index view.

ListBlock.title: ListBlock
ListBlock.description: An ordered or bulleted list. Each item supports inline markdown.

LocationReading.title: LocationReading
LocationReading.description:
A single GPS reading from a client device.

Written to ``conversations.metadata_.last_location`` and read by
the concierge's origin resolver (15-min TTL). Piggybacks on message
send requests so the concierge always sees the freshest available
location at turn time without a separate round-trip.

ManifestEntry.title: ManifestEntry
ManifestEntry.description:
One entry in the offline-narration manifest.

Carries everything the client needs to render the entry (entity
metadata, depth tier) plus the audio URL to download. The
``audio_url`` is a relative path against the API base — the client
constructs the absolute URL itself so this works across dev /
staging / prod without server-side host detection.

MapMemberStay.title: MapMemberStay
MapMemberStay.description:
A per-person stay pin (split-lodging) for the trip map.

Distinct from ``MapBase`` (the trip's single shared base, derived from
the itinerary). These come from the ``trip_accommodations`` table — one
per member who recorded their own stay. Only stays with coordinates
(free-form lat/lng) are projected; typed-entity stays without resolved
coords are skipped. The route passes a viewer-filtered list, so a
member's ``private`` stay never reaches anyone but its owner.

geometry.title: Geometry
geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon or MultiPolygon as a structured object.

MapNeighborhood.title: MapNeighborhood
MapNeighborhood.description:
A neighborhood/district polygon for the destination city.

Sourced from ``places`` rows whose ``place_type`` is one of
neighborhood | district | quarter | borough and whose ``parent_id``
matches the city-level place. Only rows with non-NULL PostGIS geometry
are included.

``geometry`` is a structured GeoJSON Polygon / MultiPolygon dict
(the ``ST_AsGeoJSON`` output parsed server-side). Earlier versions
of this contract shipped the raw JSON string under ``geojson``,
which forced clients to ``JSON.parse`` inside an already-JSON
response — wasteful for large city polygon sets.

MapPhoto.title: MapPhoto
MapPhoto.description:
A geotagged trip photo for the map photo layer (P3).

Sourced from ``trip_photos`` rows with non-null ``gps_lat`` / ``gps_lng``.
The ``cdn_url`` is extracted from the ``photo_record`` JSONB column.
Only group-visible (``visibility ∈ {group, group_and_learn}``) non-deleted
photos are included — the uploader's own ``private`` photos are excluded
from the shared map layer.

MapSavedPlace.title: MapSavedPlace
MapSavedPlace.description:
A user-saved venue near the trip destination (Layer 2 context pin).

Sourced from ``entity_saves`` joined to ``venues`` via a PostGIS proximity
query in ``backend/core/db/saves.py::list_saved_near_place``. Only venue
saves within ``radius_km`` of the destination centroid are projected; saves
for entities without resolvable geometry are skipped. Used by the map canvas
to render a soft "context" pin layer distinct from itinerary stops.

MapShareResponse.title: MapShareResponse
MapShareResponse.description:
Response for the interactive trip map share endpoint (P4).

The ``url`` is a public Mapbox GL JS preview page protected by an HMAC
token; the token is the only gate, no auth on the preview page itself.

status.title: Status
status.description: Commitment fold exposed on the map (booked | tentative | confirmed | cancelled). Plan / itinerary blocks carry richer lifecycle strings (suggested, proposed, handed_off, booking_in_progress, …); the map projection collapses those via ``_map_stop_public_status`` in ``backend/core/map_state/builder.py`` (lines 56–68).

route_geometry.title: Route Geometry
route_geometry.description: GeoJSON-derived walking tour path for experience blocks with a LINESTRING route_geometry stored in PostGIS. Each MapPoint is one vertex of the path. NULL for non-experience stops or experiences without route geometry.

MemoryHomeTripCard.title: MemoryHomeTripCard
MemoryHomeTripCard.description: Recent-trip card on the Memory home surface.

MemoryResetResponse.title: MemoryResetResponse
MemoryResetResponse.description: Counts of rows deleted per source table — useful for confirmation UI.

MoveBlockRequest.title: MoveBlockRequest
MoveBlockRequest.description:
Body for POST /api/trips/{trip_id}/itinerary/blocks/{block_id}/move.

``day_id`` may differ from the block's current day (cross-day move).
``time_start`` / ``time_end`` are ISO-8601 strings; null clears the slot.

MoveCheckResult.title: MoveCheckResult
MoveCheckResult.description:
Response for GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/itinerary/blocks/{block_id}/move-check.

``overlaps`` are hard conflicts; travel-time booleans are soft warnings.
``would_create`` (X-CRIT-2) is the Phase 3 standing-watcher scan run
against the TARGET DAY's hypothetical post-move state — so the user sees
tight_hop / over_packed / dead_gap / budget_overrun warnings BEFORE
committing the move, not after.

MoveConflict.title: MoveConflict
MoveConflict.description: A block on the target day that overlaps the proposed time window.

MuteTodayRequest.title: MuteTodayRequest
MuteTodayRequest.description:
Optional body for the mute-today endpoint.

``local_date`` is the calendar date in the user's device timezone (ISO-8601,
e.g. "2026-05-26"). When provided, the mute row is keyed on this date rather
than today in UTC, preventing the UTC-vs-local skew that caused users in
far-ahead timezones (UTC+9+) to mute the wrong day.

NarrateCitation.title: NarrateCitation
NarrateCitation.description:
One traceable claim in the narration reply (M-2).

Frontend renders the ``claim`` substring as a clickable inline
marker in the rendered reply text; tapping reveals the source
fragment by ``fragment_index`` (1-based, matching the [N] indices
Sonnet saw in its prompt).

persona_override.title: Persona Override
persona_override.description: Optional override for the persona auto-resolved from the trip's destination. Eval/admin only — production clients should leave this null.

location.description: Optional GPS reading from the client at trigger time.

session_arc.title: Session Arc
session_arc.description: Legacy ephemeral arc text. Kept for backward compatibility; new clients should pass ``session_id`` instead and let the server load the arc from ``voice_guide_sessions``. When both are set, ``session_id`` wins — the persistent arc is the source of truth.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Optional voice-guide session this narration belongs to. When set and the session is open, the server uses the session's ``arc_summary`` as the prompt context (overriding any request-supplied ``session_arc``) and appends a one-line entry after a successful narration so the next call sees this stop in the arc. Best-effort throughout — a missing or closed session falls back to the legacy ``session_arc`` behavior without surfacing an error.

NarrateRequest.title: NarrateRequest
NarrateRequest.description: A geofence-triggered or 'I'm at X' arrival event.

NarrationEntity.title: NarrationEntity
NarrationEntity.description:
The place being narrated.

``id`` is a string at the wire level so UUID-keyed entities (experiences)
and integer-keyed entities (venues, sites, places) can share the
schema. Server-side coercion to int happens before retrieval — see
``_coerce_entity_id`` below.

NarrationHistoryItem.title: NarrationHistoryItem
NarrationHistoryItem.description: One distinct narration the user has heard (Atlas replay list).

NarrationManifest.title: NarrationManifest
NarrationManifest.description:
Manifest of all pre-rendered narration audio for a trip.

Phase Voice-1 / Phase 2.5. The client fetches this on a WiFi
connection pre-departure to download every cached narration to
local storage. Once downloaded, ``useNarrationAudio`` plays from
the local path when offline.

NarrationStop.title: NarrationStop
NarrationStop.description: One navigable stop in the trip's itinerary, with geofence metadata.

NearbyItem.title: NearbyItem
NearbyItem.description: A venue or site near a given point.

NearbyResponse.title: NearbyResponse
NearbyResponse.description: Nearby venues/sites around a geographic point.

NotificationStatsResponse.title: NotificationStatsResponse
NotificationStatsResponse.description: Aggregated engagement metrics for a trip's notification outcomes.

OnboardingSignalsCreate.title: OnboardingSignalsCreate
OnboardingSignalsCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /api/users/{user_id}/onboarding-signals``.

Non-constraint taste/pace signals from the onboarding flow.

``branch`` identifies which onboarding path produced these signals.
``interest_axis`` and ``pace_axis`` are soft preference labels — never
treated as hard constraints. ``source`` identifies the onboarding version
so signals can be versioned as the onboarding flow evolves.

OptimizeRouteSuggestion.title: OptimizeRouteSuggestion
OptimizeRouteSuggestion.description:
One block's new position in the optimized day order.

Returned by the optimize-route endpoint; client applies by issuing N
move_block calls in the suggested order. Backend never commits — this
is a preview surface only.

ParagraphBlock.title: ParagraphBlock
ParagraphBlock.description: A prose paragraph. Supports inline markdown in ``text``.

PatchTripRequest.title: PatchTripRequest
PatchTripRequest.description:
User-editable trip fields.

Excludes internal fields (``status``, ``trip_summary``, ``planning_brief``)
that are managed by the concierge or background jobs and must not be
overwritten by direct client mutations.

PendingInviteAnswer.title: PendingInviteAnswer
PendingInviteAnswer.description:
An invite the organizer hasn't acknowledged yet, where the recipient
left a pre-auth chip answer.

Surfaces neutral "Sam answered privately" momentum in the bell. Cleared when the
organizer opens ``GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/invites`` (the Pending
invites surface) — that endpoint stamps ``intake_seen_at = now()``
on every invite for the trip.

PendingVote.title: PendingVote
PendingVote.description: A proposal the user hasn't voted on.

PersonaBreakdown.title: PersonaBreakdown
PersonaBreakdown.description:
Per-persona TTFT slice. ``persona_id`` is null for turns that
didn't carry a persona_id (legacy rows; new turns always set it).

PhotoUploadRequest.title: PhotoUploadRequest
PhotoUploadRequest.description: Client requests a presigned URL for an approved photo.

PlaceAnglesByLens.title: PlaceAnglesByLens
PlaceAnglesByLens.description:
Response for ``GET /api/places/{slug}/angles/by-lens``.

``lens_groups`` contains one entry per lens that has ≥ 1 published angle.
``unlensed`` holds published angles without a ``travel_lens`` value (older
content or user-query angles that weren't assigned one).

PlaceDossierItem.title: PlaceDossierItem
PlaceDossierItem.description:
A place dossier listing item, carrying its source place so a city-level
feed can show which neighborhood each dossier came from.

PlanBlockFlag.title: PlanBlockFlag
PlanBlockFlag.description: UI hints derived server-side for blocks.

PlanBookingSummary.title: PlanBookingSummary
PlanBookingSummary.description: Lightweight booking session row for the plan-state shell.

PlanCommitmentState.title: PlanCommitmentState
PlanCommitmentState.description: Server-derived commitment lane for an itinerary block.

id.title: Id
id.description: Opaque decision identifier; for ``change_proposal`` equals the change-proposal UUID (see ``_proposal_to_plan_decision``).

PlanNextItem.title: PlanNextItem
PlanNextItem.description: Next actionable moment on the timeline (best-effort).

PlanSimilarSeed.title: PlanSimilarSeed
PlanSimilarSeed.description:
Public "plan something like this" seed derived from a shared story.

Privacy-safe by construction: built only from the ``PublicTripStory``
projection, so it carries no private inputs. A viewer can fetch this
without logging in (to see what they'd be starting) — account creation
is only required to actually create the derived trip.

PlanTravelSegment.title: PlanTravelSegment
PlanTravelSegment.description: Mirrors ``BlockTransition`` — extras allowed for booking-specific keys.

PlanningIntentBootstrap.title: PlanningIntentBootstrap
PlanningIntentBootstrap.description: Onboarding trip draft fields mirrored from the mobile client.

PrerenderHintResponse.title: PrerenderHintResponse
PrerenderHintResponse.description: Response shape for the speculative pre-render endpoint.

ProactiveEvent.title: ProactiveEvent
ProactiveEvent.description: One append-only row describing autonomous agent work.

ProactiveNotification.title: ProactiveNotification
ProactiveNotification.description:
A proactive in-app notification from the notification intelligence system.

Sourced from ``notification_deliveries`` (channel='in_app') joined with
``notification_outcomes`` so the feed can surface personalised nudges
alongside conversation unread counts and proposal votes.

ProfileFactDisputeRequest.title: ProfileFactDisputeRequest
ProfileFactDisputeRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/me/profile-fact/dispute``.

Records a "not quite" reaction on a non-phrase profile fact — a
taste-dimension reading or an interest noun. Companion to
``dispute_dna_phrase``; lives in the "What I know about you"
transparency surface that disputes multiple facets from one screen.

``kind`` discriminates the observation copy. ``value`` is the
user-visible string the user just disputed (e.g. ``"Planner"`` or
``"seafood"``). ``label`` is optional context — for taste dims it
carries the dimension name (``"energy"`` / ``"planning"`` / …) so
the observation reads naturally; ignored for interests.

force.title: Force
force.description: Override the readiness gate. By default we require at least one destination and a date window in the intent scratchpad; set force=True to promote anyway (e.g. for testing).

PromoteToTripRequest.title: PromoteToTripRequest
PromoteToTripRequest.description: Explicit promotion of a pre-trip conversation into a committed trip.

ProposalDetail.title: ProposalDetail
ProposalDetail.description:
Full proposal detail.

When ``proposed_entity_type`` + ``proposed_entity_id`` are set, the
mobile app deep-links the proposal card into that entity's detail
view, where a per-viewer personalized Take is fetched via
``POST /api/takes``. Both are null for proposals that don't point
at a single entity (e.g. pure reschedule, "add a day").

``voting_enabled`` mirrors the trip-level flag — the mobile app uses
it to decide whether to render the vote widget (enabled) or a
reaction-only view with an "ask organizer to resolve" affordance
(disabled).

ProposalImpactAnalysis.title: ProposalImpactAnalysis
ProposalImpactAnalysis.description:
Structured trade-off payload for a change proposal.

All fields are optional so the concierge can fill in as much or as
little as warranted by the proposal stakes. ``extra="allow"`` preserves
legacy keys (e.g. ``new_time``, ``new_title``, ``new_notes``) written
by older proposals and consumed by ``proposal_apply._apply_*``.

ProposalSummary.title: ProposalSummary
ProposalSummary.description:
Lightweight proposal for list views.

Deliberately omits ``proposed_entity_*`` — the deep-link affordance
is a detail-page concern, not a list-row concern.

``my_vote`` is the authenticated viewer's current vote ("approve",
"reject", "neutral") or ``None`` if they haven't voted yet. The
mobile client uses this to filter the "Needs your vote" digest.

ProviderBreakdown.title: ProviderBreakdown
ProviderBreakdown.description:
Per-STT-provider TTFT slice (V-3). Surfaces the latency cost of
the on-device fallback (Apple SpeechAnalyzer / Picovoice) vs. the
cloud baseline (Deepgram). Once the iOS native module ships and
starts emitting ``stt_provider != 'deepgram_cloud'``, this is the
surface that lights up to compare them.

PublicProfile.title: PublicProfile
PublicProfile.description:
Public-safe view of another traveler — what a viewer sees before they
follow. DEFAULT-DENY: taste_phrases are the display-safe concierge phrases
(never hard constraints / budget / health), and stories are only the
owner's follower/link-visible ones.

PublicProfileStory.title: PublicProfileStory
PublicProfileStory.description: A public-safe story summary on a profile — links to the public page.

PublicReason.title: PublicReason
PublicReason.description: A reason that is safe to show in any channel.

PublicStorySection.title: PublicStorySection
PublicStorySection.description: A public-safe narrative section — body only, no internal ids.

PublicTripStory.title: PublicTripStory
PublicTripStory.description:
The PUBLIC projection of a Trip Story.

DEFAULT-DENY ALLOWLIST. This model is the *complete* set of fields a
non-owner may ever see. It deliberately omits ``user_id``, ``trip_id``,
``version``, section ids, ``anchor_observation_id``, ``edited_at``,
``composer_model``, and every private input (constraints, budget,
health, fatigue, observations). Build it ONLY via
``backend.core.story_projection.build_public_story_payload`` so the
allowlist is enforced in exactly one place.

ReactRequest.title: ReactRequest
ReactRequest.description: React to a structured message (reaction_card or vote_widget).

move.title: Move
move.description: One-line verdict — 'the move is X because Y'. Opinionated.

why_for_group.title: Why For Group
why_for_group.description: Why this fits THIS group (energy, constraints, dynamic).

why_for_person.title: Why For Person
why_for_person.description: Why this fits the requester specifically (personal memory, prior preferences).

what_to_notice.title: What To Notice
what_to_notice.description: Connoisseur notes — what to look for, order, ask about.

what_to_skip.title: What To Skip
what_to_skip.description: What to avoid or deprioritize at this place.

timing_note.title: Timing Note
timing_note.description: When to go, how long, reservation tip, time-of-day cue.

RecommendationPayload.title: RecommendationPayload
RecommendationPayload.description: Structured reasoning attached to a concierge recommendation.

accuracy_m.title: Accuracy M
accuracy_m.description: Reported GPS accuracy in meters; null when client can't supply it.

RecordGeofenceEventRequest.title: RecordGeofenceEventRequest
RecordGeofenceEventRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/trips/{trip_id}/geofence-events``.

The ``user_id`` is taken from auth — clients can only record their
own events. Attempting to specify someone else would be a privacy
leak and is rejected at the application layer regardless.

RecordGeofenceEventResponse.title: RecordGeofenceEventResponse
RecordGeofenceEventResponse.description:
Composite response: the event row + the session it's bound to.

When ``event_type='entered'`` and no session is active, the route
opens one and returns it inline so the client doesn't need a second
round-trip to learn its session_id. When the caller enters a different
entity, the prior session is closed with ``close_reason='next_place'``
by ``open_session`` and a fresh arc opens. Otherwise ``session`` is
null (the trigger was recorded but no session changes resulted) or
the existing active session.

RedactionPolicy.title: RedactionPolicy
RedactionPolicy.description:
Owner-controlled toggles for what the PUBLIC projection may reveal.

Default-deny: a viewer sees only what these toggles explicitly allow,
on top of the always-public artifact (hero, narrative, chosen photos).
Exact dates are OFF by default; the destination headline is on (it's
the point of the share) but can be turned off.

RegisterDeviceRequest.title: RegisterDeviceRequest
RegisterDeviceRequest.description: Mobile client calls this on login / app start to register its push token.

RelatedDossierItem.title: RelatedDossierItem
RelatedDossierItem.description: Lightweight dossier stub for the Keep Reading carousel.

ResolveRequest.title: ResolveRequest
ResolveRequest.description:
Resolve (accept/reject) a change proposal.

The resolver is the authenticated actor — they must be an
organizer of the trip. No ``resolved_by`` in the body; trusting
the client to name the resolver would bypass authz.

RevertRequest.title: RevertRequest
RevertRequest.description:
Revert an already-accepted proposal, restoring the prior itinerary state.

Only the trip organizer may revert. The ``resolution_note`` is stored on
the proposal row and surfaced in the group chat bridge message so members
understand why the change was undone.

``mode`` selects the revert strategy. ``None``/``"diff_safe"`` keeps the
surgical reverse-patch that preserves unrelated later edits (and may return
``diff_safe_partial`` when some fields were edited since). ``"version_restore"``
is the "Force full revert" path the conflict sheet offers — it snaps the
itinerary back to the pre-accept version, discarding later edits. Previously
this field was silently dropped by FastAPI, so "Force full revert" was a
no-op for any modern (post-Phase-2) proposal.

RouteRequest.title: RouteRequest
RouteRequest.description: Inputs to the modality routing decision.

SearchInterpretation.title: SearchInterpretation
SearchInterpretation.description:
Structured interpretation of a search query, generated by Vesper (Haiku).

``label`` is a 3-7 word natural-language restatement of the user's intent
(suitable for display above results).  ``mode`` distinguishes open-ended
vibes queries (``"intent"``) from specific lookup queries (``"lookup"``).
``entity_focus`` is the primary entity type the query targets.

city.title: City
city.description: Destination slug scope (e.g. 'lisbon'). Omit for global search.

experience_type.title: Experience Type
experience_type.description: concert, theater, comedy, festival, tour, etc.

entity_types.title: Entity Types
entity_types.description: Entity types to search: ["venue", "experience", "site", "accommodation", "angle", "dossier"]. "event" is a legacy alias for "experience". Defaults to all.

SearchRequest.title: SearchRequest
SearchRequest.description: Unified search request — drives the Discover tab search bar.

SearchResponse.title: SearchResponse
SearchResponse.description: Unified search response with fused results.

SearchResultItem.title: SearchResultItem
SearchResultItem.description: A single search result — venue, experience, site, or accommodation.

SerializedActionReceipt.title: SerializedActionReceipt
SerializedActionReceipt.description:
What the API returns after channel-based filtering.

private_influences are never present — only safe_label values are
surfaced via the public_reasons fallback mechanism.

SessionInfoResponse.title: SessionInfoResponse
SessionInfoResponse.description: Basic info about the current session.

SetPhoneRequest.title: SetPhoneRequest
SetPhoneRequest.description: E.164 format (e.g. +14155551234). Pass null to clear.

SettleBatchItem.title: SettleBatchItem
SettleBatchItem.description: One leg of a batch settlement — settle ``user_ids`` on ``expense_id``.

SettleBatchRequest.title: SettleBatchRequest
SettleBatchRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/trips/{trip_id}/settle-batch``.

Each item carries the same authorization contract as the single-expense
settle endpoint; the batch path simply replaces N round-trips with one
when the UI settles a payer's whole row at once.

SettleBatchResponse.title: SettleBatchResponse
SettleBatchResponse.description: All updated expenses, in request order.

ShareStats.title: ShareStats
ShareStats.description: Owner-facing share metrics. Aggregate counts only — never viewer identity.

ShareView.title: ShareView
ShareView.description: Owner-facing view of a share — what the app shows after publishing.

SituationAmbientNearbyCandidate.title: SituationAmbientNearbyCandidate
SituationAmbientNearbyCandidate.description: Backend-authored nearby suggestion for ambient trip surfaces.

SituationAttention.title: SituationAttention
SituationAttention.description: The top attention candidates derivable without running an LLM.

SituationGroup.title: SituationGroup
SituationGroup.description: Group context safe for shared/app surfaces.

SituationPlanBlock.title: SituationPlanBlock
SituationPlanBlock.description: Compact reference to the block most relevant right now.

SituationTimeline.title: SituationTimeline
SituationTimeline.description: Where the trip is on its timeline and what is next.

SituationVoiceSession.title: SituationVoiceSession
SituationVoiceSession.description: Public-safe summary of the active voice-guide arc.

SourceRef.title: SourceRef
SourceRef.description: A piece of evidence or system event that caused the action.

SpeculativeAngle.title: SpeculativeAngle
SpeculativeAngle.description:
A non-persisted angle generated on the fly from a user query.

These are suggestions only — no ``id``, no dossier.  The frontend can
display them as fast-start discovery chips ("Want to explore this angle?")
and the user can trigger a proper research run from there.

SpineReceipt.title: SpineReceipt
SpineReceipt.description: Spine — day-shape progress, not a completeness gauge (Standing floor).

StorySharePreview.title: StorySharePreview
StorySharePreview.description:
Dry-run of a share before publishing (the Privacy Preview payload).

``public`` is exactly what a viewer would see; the counts/flags let the
UI explain the privacy state in plain language without re-implementing
the redaction rules.

SuggestResponse.title: SuggestResponse
SuggestResponse.description: Autocomplete suggestions for the search bar.

TakeGenerationRequest.title: TakeGenerationRequest
TakeGenerationRequest.description: Body for POST /api/takes — request to generate (or fetch cached) a Take.

body.title: Body
body.description: Ordered list of content blocks. Paragraphs are the default; use headings only when the Take has 3+ distinct sections; use lists when enumerating 3+ items that read poorly as prose.

TakePayload.title: TakePayload
TakePayload.description:
The full Take content — what gets rendered (and what gets logged).

Tier 1 (Curator) and Tier 2 (Personal) share this shape. Personal Takes
include grounded `hypotheses` with real evidence refs; Curator Takes
leave `hypotheses` empty.

TakeResponse.title: TakeResponse
TakeResponse.description: Response shape for non-streaming Take requests.

TransportGap.title: TransportGap
TransportGap.description: One inter-city transition with no realistic transport plan.

TripBudgetUpdate.title: TripBudgetUpdate
TripBudgetUpdate.description:
Body for PATCH /api/trips/{trip_id}/budget.

All fields optional with no default — the route uses ``model_fields_set``
to distinguish "clear it" (``null``) from "not provided" (absent). A
field that the caller didn't include is left untouched on the trip.

TripContext.title: TripContext
TripContext.description: Trip-scoped context for filtering and relevance.

TripDebriefSubmit.title: TripDebriefSubmit
TripDebriefSubmit.description: Structured post-trip debrief — funnels explicit traveler signal into memory.

TripDigest.title: TripDigest
TripDigest.description:
A daily, trip-summary, or planning-context structured digest.

`content` is the LLM's structured JSON output; `rendered_markdown` is a
pre-formatted textual view populated at generation time so consumers
(planner prompt, admin UI) don't need to re-render the payload.

TripHero.title: TripHero
TripHero.description:
The typed hero payload attached to ``Trip.hero`` (not persisted).

``kind``/``severity`` are routing/register hints; ``identity``/``read``/
``receipt``/``step`` are the renderable content. Live/time-pressure states
(Orient/Urgent/Ready) override this from their own FE hooks rather than the
trips-list field.

phase.title: Phase
phase.description: Trip-status-relative phase for map UX: ideation | planning | final_prep | active | completed (from ``Trip.status`` via ``_TRIP_STATUS_TO_PHASE`` in the map-state builder). This answers “where is the trip in its lifecycle?” — distinct from ``HomeAnchor.phase`` on the trip-home feed, which is calendar-relative (ideation | pre | during | post) from ``compute_phase_info`` over today's date vs trip dates.

saved_nearby.title: Saved Nearby
saved_nearby.description: User-saved venues within ~5 km of the destination centroid (Layer 2). Empty when the trip has no destination or the user has no saves near it. Sourced from ``entity_saves`` JOIN ``venues`` via ST_DWithin.

neighborhoods.title: Neighborhoods
neighborhoods.description: Neighborhood/district polygons for the destination city, sourced from ``places`` rows with polygon geometry. Empty list when no destination is set or the destination has no child neighborhood rows. Used by the frontend to render a semi-transparent fill layer (Layer 3).

photos.title: Photos
photos.description: Geotagged trip photos with GPS coordinates (P3 photo layer). Empty when no photos have been uploaded and geotagged. Visibility-filtered: only group / group_and_learn photos are returned.

TripPhoto.title: TripPhoto
TripPhoto.description: One row from trip_photos as returned by the API.

TripPhotoBlockPatch.title: TripPhotoBlockPatch
TripPhotoBlockPatch.description: Body for PATCH /{photo_id}/block — manual block assignment / removal.

TripStoryPhotoSlot.title: TripStoryPhotoSlot
TripStoryPhotoSlot.description: Placeholder where a photo will land. photo_url is set when the user picks one.

TripStoryPhotoSlotEdit.title: TripStoryPhotoSlotEdit
TripStoryPhotoSlotEdit.description: Body of a PATCH to a single photo slot. Pass null to clear.

TripStorySection.title: TripStorySection
TripStorySection.description: A single addressable narrative section of a Trip Story.

TripStorySectionEdit.title: TripStorySectionEdit
TripStorySectionEdit.description: Body of a PATCH to a single section.

TripSummaryResponse.title: TripSummaryResponse
TripSummaryResponse.description: AI-generated retrospective for a completed trip.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: URL of the image to rehost.

UserFact.title: UserFact
UserFact.description:
A single structured fact about a traveler.

Supersession forms a linked list: a fact with ``valid_to`` set AND
``superseded_by`` pointing at a newer fact means "I now know better".
``valid_to`` set with ``superseded_by`` null means "retired without
replacement" (forget_fact).

UserFactsResponse.title: UserFactsResponse
UserFactsResponse.description: Active user facts grouped by namespace, plus the namespace catalog.

UserMapResponse.title: UserMapResponse
UserMapResponse.description: GET /api/me/map response — all mapped venues across all trips.

UserMapTrip.title: UserMapTrip
UserMapTrip.description:
One trip that contributed at least one mapped venue.

The frontend keys pin color off ``trip_id`` and looks up trip metadata
(title, dates, status) here, so the per-venue payload stays small.

UserMapVenue.title: UserMapVenue
UserMapVenue.description:
A single placed stop on the cross-trip map.

``block_id`` is the itinerary block id (stable per stop) — venues can
recur across trips, so the block id is the right de-dup/key handle.
Only blocks that resolved to real coordinates appear here.

UserMeResponse.title: UserMeResponse
UserMeResponse.description: Response for GET /api/me — full profile data for the Me tab.

UserMessageResponse.title: UserMessageResponse
UserMessageResponse.description: Response when a message is persisted without triggering an agent reply.

UserOpenProposalSummary.title: UserOpenProposalSummary
UserOpenProposalSummary.description: Open proposal row with trip context for the global unresolved bar.

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description: Partial update — only include fields you want to change.

VenueBriefItem.title: VenueBriefItem
VenueBriefItem.description: Compact venue brief — the 20% slot in the global Discover feed.

VenueDetailResponse.title: VenueDetailResponse
VenueDetailResponse.description:
Shape returned by GET /api/venues/{venue_id}.

Mirrors ``APIVenueDetail`` in the frontend types.

Verdict.title: Verdict
Verdict.description:
Opinion layer. Derived from signals, not free LLM judgment.

`derived_from` lists the signal categories the verdict is grounded in,
for traceability and debugging.

VoiceGuideSession.title: VoiceGuideSession
VoiceGuideSession.description:
One arc the user moved through, anchored at an opening entity.

A session opens when narration starts at a stop and stays open while
the user moves through related stops (same place, same angle ladder)
within a rolling window. ``arc_summary`` is the running text the next
narration call appends to and feeds back to the LLM. ``closed_at``
lands when a new place is entered or the user goes idle past a
threshold.

VoiceHandoffRequest.title: VoiceHandoffRequest
VoiceHandoffRequest.description: Request for an interrupted-narration voice session.

VoiceHandoffResponse.title: VoiceHandoffResponse
VoiceHandoffResponse.description: Token + context for opening an interrupted-narration voice session.

VoiceMetricsWindow.title: VoiceMetricsWindow
VoiceMetricsWindow.description:
Echoed-back window + sample size so the caller knows what they
actually got — important when the requested window had no data.

VoicePersonaInfo.title: VoicePersonaInfo
VoicePersonaInfo.description:
Persona metadata surfaced to the client so the UI can render the
persona name + voice provenance during a voice session.

Phase 4.6. The client uses this to show "Talking with Mateus" in the
voice overlay and to know which voice (id) the TTS will use. The
client doesn't need full persona spec — just identity for display.

VoiceTokenRequest.title: VoiceTokenRequest
VoiceTokenRequest.description:
Request a LiveKit access token for voice chat.

B1 audit fix: ``user_id`` and ``user_role`` are no longer accepted
from the request body — they're sourced from the authenticated
actor instead. Previously a missing-auth handler let anyone mint a
LiveKit JWT as any user for any trip by sending arbitrary body
fields. The request only carries display metadata now.

VoiceTokenResponse.title: VoiceTokenResponse
VoiceTokenResponse.description: LiveKit connection credentials for the client.

WeekRibbonReceipt.title: WeekRibbonReceipt
WeekRibbonReceipt.description: WeekRibbon — the lived week, one cell per day (Returned · Shape).

backend__api__routes__atlas__ApproveRequ.title: ApproveRequest
backend__api__routes__atlas__ApproveRequ.description:
Optional body for ``POST /api/atlas/candidates/{id}/approve``.

``style`` selects the Vesper narrative voice used during artifact
generation. Defaults to ``"memory"`` (the warm, literary default).
Other values: ``"postcard"`` (Instagram-native), ``"journal"``
(first-person, introspective), ``"dispatch"`` (editorial, thesis-driven).

``reflection`` is a short, optional user-authored note ("what was
happening / what did this feel like") captured at approval time. The
typing itself is the load-bearing memory-consolidation moment per the
photo-taking-impairment research; persisting it gives the artifact
detail a "back of the postcard" surface.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: MIME type — Anthropic vision accepts JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF.

backend__api__routes__chat__MessageImage.title: MessageImage
backend__api__routes__chat__MessageImage.description:
Inline image attached to a chat message.

The Anthropic chat API accepts image content blocks in messages —
we forward the base64 + mime type through the agent loop so the
concierge can see what the user is asking about ("what is this
monument?", "is this dish vegetarian?", scan a paper menu).

Sized for mobile: clients should compress to <=1MB base64 (typical
JPEG at 1920px wide, quality 0.7 = 150-300KB). Larger images flow
through the existing receipt-upload path instead.

Mirrors the receipt-OCR call shape (``backend/expenses/receipt_ocr.py``)
so the same vision-capable code path serves both surfaces.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Written to conversation metadata before the turn runs; read by the concierge's distance tool when from_origin='current_location'.

images.title: Images
images.description: Optional images to attach to this message. The concierge answers questions about what's in the photo (translate a menu, identify a monument, scan an entry sign). Multiple images are concatenated in order. Limit 4 per message.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional structured per-turn metadata from the client (e.g. {'angle_id': 42} when entering from the angle detail screen). Persisted on the user message row and surfaced to the agent.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: A single message from a user to the concierge.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: What the concierge returns after processing a message.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: MIME type accepted by the concierge vision path.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Mes.title: MessageImage
backend__api__routes__conversations__Mes.description: Inline image attached to a conversation-scoped Vesper turn.

trigger_agent.title: Trigger Agent
trigger_agent.description: If False, persist the message without triggering an agent reply. Useful for user-to-user chat in group conversations.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Stored on the conversation's metadata before the turn runs.

images.title: Images
images.description: Optional images to attach to this turn. Mirrors the trip-scoped chat endpoint so private Vesper chats do not silently drop photo context.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional turn metadata from the client. Used for structured entry-point grounding such as metadata.conversation_seed.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Send a message in a conversation.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Agent reply after processing a message.
